author: pk910
content:
dora gets the next canonical block that builds on top of the current block and checks if the parent execution block hash matches the current execution payload hash.
that might change if dora sees another chain as canonical.

so yea, the details page gets it right,  but the list view is wrong

author: potuz
content: ah so the details page is the one that gets actualized when the chain changes the canonical view?

author: potuz
content: I should trust more the details page in general?

author: potuz
content: for older blocks at least right?

author: pk910
content: both change.  the logic should be equal 😄   let me check 🙂

author: potuz
content: ah yeah it shows differently now

author: nflaig
content: lodestar-geth-1 managed to sync from genesis, we should be voting properly on all nodes now, thanks for checking that

author: potuz
content: beautiful and bad at the same time, we have a bug that makes us propose on full when empty should have won, I think <@363800010518822915> is investigating it, but now triggering it will be harder 🙂

author: potuz
content: ping on this one, this is something that the spec is rather lacking. It's one of the odd consequences of having to deal with payload equivocations

author: terencechain
content: Hi <@&1417820113981145228> has anyone run into the following scenario?  When calling `is_parent_block_full` on the beacon state at the first Gloas slot, the function returns true even though the last Fulu block had no full payload. This happens because `UpgradeToGloas` initializes both `latest_execution_payload_bid.block_hash` and `latest_block_hash` to the same (the last Fulu EL hash), so the equality check in is_parent_block_full is satisfied spuriously.  Does this  break anything for your internal caching

author: potuz
content: We designed that function so that it returns true at the fork

author: pawandhananjay
content: <@737658693331714159> ran into this issue when working on fork choice i think

author: nc1234
content: Yes `is_parent_block_full` should be true at first gloas slot. Our internal caching treats all pre-gloas blocks to be FULL variants anyways

author: hopinheimer
content: Yup I’m assuming all pre-Gloas blocks to be FULL as well. Since we have variants for ProtoNode.

author: potuz
content: we have some sort of a hybrid because we didn't want to change the fact that we use blockroots to access pre Gloas poststates

author: potuz
content: so we still treat them as empty

author: potuz
content: but in some places they are naturally treated as full

author: potuz
content: We could just move to treat them entirely as full, but that seemed like a more dangerous change to merge now in develop
